text: I'll help you create GitHub issues for each merged pull request that closes a Linear issue. Let me start by exploring the database structure to understand what tables are available.

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT table_name FROM information_schema.tables ORDER BY table_name"}

text: Now let me examine the structure of the relevant tables, particularly `github_pull_requests` and `linear_issues`:

tool_calls.name: execute_sql

tool_calls.name: execute_sql

text: Perfect! I can see that `github_pull_requests` has a `closes_linear` column that seems to indicate which Linear issue a PR closes. Now I need to find all MERGED pull requests that close a Linear issue, then create GitHub issues in the 'acme…(+98)

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT COUNT(*) as total_prs, \n       COUNT(CASE WHEN state = 'MERGED' AND closes_linear IS NOT NULL THEN 1 END) as merged_closing_linear\nFROM github_pull_requests"}

text: Interesting! There are 80 pull requests total, but none that are both merged AND close a Linear issue. Let me check if there are any pull requests that close Linear issues at all, regardless of state:

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input:
{"sql":"SELECT number, repo, title, state, closes_linear 
FROM github_pull_requests 
WHERE closes_linear IS NOT NULL 
ORDER BY number"}
